chapter_title.en: Chapter on Eating and Drinking From Gold and Silver Utensils and Other Etiquettes of Eating

parts.text: يُوسُفَ أَخِيهِ : أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اِسْتَسْقَى مَاءً فَأُتِيَ بِقَدَحٍ مِنْ صُفْرٍ

text: هِ مَاءٌ فَقَالَ لَهُ بَعْضُ جُلَسَائِهِ إِنَّ عَبَّادَ اَلْبَصْرِيَّ يَكْرَهُ اَلشُّرْبَ فِي اَلصُّفْرِ قَالَ "فَسَلْهُ أَ ذَهَبٌ هُوَ أَمْ فِضَّةٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4240 - Yunus bin Yaqub narrated from his brother Yusuf:
Abu Abdullah (as), once asked for water, and a vessel made of brass containing water was brought to him.
One of those sitting with him said: "'Abbad al-Basri dislikes drinking from brass vessels."
Imam (as) said: "Then ask him, is it gold or silver?"
